title: phebalium rude subsp. lineare
intro: Phebalium rude subsp. lineare, also known as the Coastal Baeckea or Yellow Baeckea, is a shrub native to the eastern coastal regions of Australia, characterized by its narrow, linear leaves and yellow flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. It is believed to have health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. Coastal Baeckea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and provide relief from various health conditions.
